findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Over-broad description and keyword baiting
findings.description: The skill description contains an extensive list of trigger keywords and phrases designed to maximize activation across a wide range of user requests. This is a form of keyword baiting that could lead to unwanted or excessive activation of the skill.
findings.evidence: Description includes: 'create a hook', 'add a PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop hook', 'validate tool use', 'implement prompt-based hooks', 'use ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}', 'set up event-driven automation', 'block dangerous commands', 'hook events (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, SubagentStop, SessionStart, SessionEnd, UserPromptSubmit, PreCompact, Notification)'
findings.remediation: Narrow the description to focus on the core functionality rather than listing every possible trigger phrase.

findings.title: Missing allowed-tools field in manifest
findings.description: The YAML frontmatter does not specify an 'allowed-tools' field. While this is optional per the spec, its absence means there is no declared restriction on which agent tools this skill may use.
findings.evidence: YAML frontmatter: name: hook-development, description: ..., version: 0.1.0. No allowed-tools field present.
findings.remediation: Consider adding an 'allowed-tools' field to the manifest to declare which agent tools the skill is intended to use.

findings.title: External resource link without version pinning
findings.description: The skill references external documentation at 'https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/hooks' and suggests using 'jq' for validation. While not malicious, external dependencies should be version-pinned or have clear provenance.
findings.evidence: External Resources section: 'Official Docs: https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/hooks' and 'Validation: Use jq to validate hook JSON output'
findings.remediation: Pin external tool versions where possible and document the specific versions tested.
